framing: Is the machine spending its resources on PRODUCT or on ITSELF? This attributes elapsed TIME (the utilisation denominator, from git commit timestamps) and TOKENS (the cost denominator, from the token log) -- SEPARATELY, because they diverge and the divergence is signal -- to a productive/waste activity taxonomy built entirely from data this repo already keeps. A reporting layer, not new instrumentation.
guardrail: DIAGNOSTIC, NEVER A TARGET (R12 / G5 law). 40% productive-and-correct beats 90% busy-and-wrong. No figure here is a completion gate or a thing to hit -- tuning the machine to make utilisation look good corrupts the very actuals it reports.
generated_from: git log (commit subjects + changed files) + docs/observability/token-log.md + action_needed_register.json

productive_pct.note: time = utilisation denominator (git clock); cost = token denominator (token log). They are tracked separately because they diverge -- the divergence is the signal, not an error.

cost_of_self_maintenance.note: Must be trendable DOWN over windows, or the harness is a treadmill.

value_per_problem.note: productive commits per PROBLEM commit (self-repair + rework + hit-limit). No problems in-window -> ratio null, not infinity.

items.what: GOVERNANCE CONFLICT (deeper finding). Two director-authored mechanisms CONTRADICT on who may cross the BUILD-open (loop_stage idle->build) gate: (A) DIRECTOR_CANON.md §3a -- 'BUILD authorization within an open epoch... explicitly delegated to the TWIN' (the twin's route_blocking_decision approved every BUILD-open I did this session on this basis). (B) fronts.yaml stage_advance gate + gate_authorization.py self-sustain-vs-self-promote -- idle->build is a RESERVED gate only a DIRECTOR CONSOLE ACT (a BUILD_OPEN in the ledger) crosses; the loop self-sustains on already-open atoms but NEVER self-promotes idle->build. (B) is the ENFORCED model -- its reconciler wedged the publish gate on my newest self-promotion (G4, now reverted). Under (B) the twin only SEQUENCES among director-BUILD_OPEN'd atoms; it does NOT authorize the flip. So this session's twin-opened builds (H26, H24_precommit, H23, H24_worktree, G4) were self-promotions with NO director BUILD_OPEN. You RATIFIED H26+H24_precommit (retroactive); G4 reverted; H23+H24_worktree stand un-flagged (baseline-grandfathered) but were opened the same improper way. SEPARATELY: the authorized-build set is now EMPTY -- every director-BUILD_OPEN'd atom is done (E4, OPS1_transport, OPS1_watchdog) or blocked (D5=payment triad, OPS1_rebuild=systemd deploy, W1_3-6/C13=deps on the un-opened W1_2). The loop has no authorized build work.
